candidates.name: Mike Braun
candidates.notes: 52nd Governor of Indiana (inaugurated Jan 2025). Former US Senator (2019-2025). Won governor's race by 13.3% — highest margin in open-seat IN governor race since 1980. Heritage Action 96% (117th), 100% (118th) as senator. Catholic. Opposes ACA, same-sex marriage, abortion, amnesty. Ranked among most conservative senators. Endorsed by Americans for Prosperity, CPAC. Executive orders banning gender-affirming care for minors, banning biological males from girls' sports, banning CRT/gender ideology discussions in public schools. Self-made businessman (Meyer Distributing). NRA excellent ratings throughout Senate tenure. Family Research Council, National Right to Life, Susan B. Anthony List all gave top ratings.

profile.education: Wabash College (BA, Economics, summa cum laude); Harvard Business School (MBA)
profile.background: Businessman who built a national auto parts distribution company; U.S. Senator (2019-2025); became 52nd governor in January 2025.

candidates.name: Todd Young
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from IN. Heritage Action LIFETIME 60% — session scores vary widely: 52% (113th as Rep), 58% (114th), 70% (115th), 68% (117th), 35% (118th), 94% (119th current). Marine veteran. More establishment-leaning Republican — co-authored CHIPS Act with bipartisan support (hence lower scores some sessions). NRA A+ rating (100% in 2018). Protestant (unspecified denomination per Pew Research). Served on Senate Foreign Relations and Finance committees. Co-authored bipartisan AI legislation. Dipped to 35% Heritage score in 118th Congress likely due to bipartisan votes.

profile.education: United States Naval Academy (BS), University of Chicago (MBA), Indiana University Robert H. McKinney School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Marine Corps veteran and former intelligence officer. Management consultant at Heritage Foundation. Former U.S. House member. In Senate since 2017. Co-authored CHIPS and Science Act.

candidates.name: Jim Banks
candidates.notes: Junior Senator since Jan 2025 (formerly US Rep IN-3, 2017-2025). Heritage Action 100% (119th Senate), 100% (116th House), 94% (118th House). One of the MOST conservative members of Congress. Navy Reserve officer (deployed to Afghanistan). Chaired Republican Study Committee. Opposed Respect for Marriage Act — argued it violated religious freedom. Opposed student loan forgiveness. Supported repealing ACA. Strong on religious freedom. NRA 'A' rating. Pro-life, defund Planned Parenthood. Applauded SCOTUS religious freedom decisions. Anti-woke, anti-CRT crusader. Columbia City, Indiana native. Evangelical Christian background.

profile.education: Indiana University (BA Political Science), Grace College & Seminary (MBA)
profile.background: Navy Reserve veteran who served in Afghanistan. Former Indiana State Senator and U.S. House member. Worked in real estate and construction before entering politics.

district_offices.address: 1300 S. Harrison St., E Ross Adair Federal Building
5400 Federal Plaza, Suite 3200, Hammond Federal Building Courthouse
300 E. Fall Creek Pkwy N Dr., Suite 300
